input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the **`BindingContext`**. Without it, `{Binding}` has nothing to resolve against.

**Set it in code-behind:**
```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

**Or in XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
```

Also verify your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit) so the UI updates when properties change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values. Looking at their XAML, the issue is clear — they haven't set the `BindingContext`.

There could also be a secondary issue: the ViewModel properties may not implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, but the most obvious problem from the XAML alone is the missing `BindingContext`.
